meta.description: Writes the full Results section of a biomedical manuscript from a sufficiently clear result structure, figure inventory, or analysis summary while preserving evidence boundaries and result hierarchy.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated results, figures, cohort details, PMIDs, or DOIs produced. Citation-support annotation provides PubMed queries only. Hard rule 10 explicitly prevents hiding missing coherence behind polished prose.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic or prescriptive conclusions. Hard rule 5 prevents Discussion-style interpretation from entering Results prose. Section G (Claim Boundary Check) enforces evidence-level constraints in the draft.

methodological_ground.detail: Hard rule 4 prevents promotion of exploratory analyses. Hard rule 6 prevents implying stronger evidence than results support. Section C (Writing Readiness Decision) enforces pre-write hierarchy check.

code_usability.detail: Mode A skill — no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Full Results prose writing from structure. Ten study types covered. Nine-step workflow + nine-section output (A–I). Section C three-way writing readiness decision (ready/partially ready/not ready) is a unique functional feature. Citation annotation with opt-out. Handoff-to-structurer when hierarchy unclear.

reliability.note: Full marks. Three independent paths when input insufficient: ask questions, upload recommendation, redirect to Results Section Structurer. Section C three-way fork makes the decision logic explicit. Section H (remaining uncertainty) and Section I (upstream recommendation) provide dual follow-up mechanisms.

performance_context.note: Eight compact reference files. SKILL.md 268 lines. Minor deduction: Section G (Claim Boundary Check) and the inline prose boundary rules create partial content overlap — both the prose itself and the explicit check state what the draft must not imply.

agent_usability.note: Full marks. Five sample triggers and quality standard comparison. Nine fixed A–I labels. Section I (Upstream Skill Recommendation) is a unique output section that no other Academic Writing skill reviewed has. Comprehensive error prevention: clarification-first, ten hard rules, 'not for' list, and 'important distinctions'.

human_usability.note: Full marks. Section C three-way fork is highly transparent — user sees explicitly whether skill will proceed, ask for more, or redirect. Section H + upload recommendation + Section I provide three forgiveness mechanisms. Quality standard comparison (strong vs. weak output) is clear and instructive.

security.note: No credentials, APIs, or code execution. Hard rules 1 and 7 prevent fabricating results or PMIDs. Hard rule 2 prevents long prose generation from vague input. Hard rule 10 prevents hiding incoherence behind polished text — the most dangerous AI writing failure mode.

maintainability.note: Eight focused reference files; handoff-to-structurer-rule.md is a unique composability reference. Adding new writing patterns requires only updating full-results-writing-rules.md. Minor deduction: no worked example of a complete Results draft for any specific study type in reference files.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision: five specific triggers plus 'not for' scoping. Progressive disclosure: Section A + Section C three-way fork + Section I upstream recommendation — best progressive disclosure in the set. Composability: explicit handoff-to-structurer-rule.md + Section I (3/4 — lacks downstream composability with discussion-composer). Idempotency: A–I structure stable. Escape hatches: Section H + upload recommendation + structurer handoff + Section I — four escape mechanisms (4/4).

inputs.label: Cohort study with approved 5-figure Results structure, requesting full prose draft
inputs.note: All five assertions passed. Section C correctly states 'ready for full Results writing'. Disciplined prose preserves figure hierarchy. Discussion drift absent.

assertions.text: Section C states 'ready for full Results writing' given the approved structure
assertions.note: Writing readiness decision correctly proceeds given sufficient figure hierarchy input.

assertions.text: Full Results draft in Section D preserves the primary → support → validation order
assertions.note: full-results-writing-rules.md applied — primary findings visible, support analyses in support position.

assertions.text: Output does not contain Discussion-style interpretation in the prose
assertions.note: results-boundary-rules.md applied throughout — observed findings only, no speculative language.

assertions.text: Citation-support markers and PubMed queries added for context-setting statements
assertions.note: Hard rule 8 applied — markers present for cohort characterization context statements.

assertions.text: Section G (Claim Boundary Check) explicitly states what the draft must not imply
assertions.note: Observational design noted — no causal claims allowed in prose.

inputs.label: Partial Results draft with Discussion-style interpretation embedded; user requests clean rewrite
inputs.note: All five assertions passed. Discussion drift correctly identified and removed from rewrite. Evidence-only language applied.

assertions.text: Output identifies Discussion-style sentences in the original draft as boundary violations
assertions.note: Section B and Section C explicitly list overinterpretive sentences before rewriting.

assertions.text: Rewritten draft removes interpretive language and replaces with observed-finding language
assertions.note: results-boundary-rules.md applied — 'these findings suggest clinical significance' → 'X was observed in Y% of patients'.

assertions.text: Output does not introduce new interpretive drift in the rewrite
assertions.note: Hard rule 5 holds throughout the rewrite — no new Discussion content introduced.

assertions.text: Section F explains why specific interpretive phrases were removed
assertions.note: Writing logic explanation covers each major boundary correction with rationale.

assertions.text: Section G states which evidence-level claims were restrained in the rewrite
assertions.note: Claim boundary check explicitly names the mechanistic interpretation that was moved to Discussion.

inputs.label: User provides only study topic with no figures, structure, or result hierarchy
inputs.note: All five assertions passed. Section C correctly states 'not ready — should use Results Section Structurer first'. No fabricated prose produced.

assertions.text: Section C correctly routes to 'not ready — use Results Section Structurer first'
assertions.note: handoff-to-structurer-rule.md applied — framed as improvement recommendation, not refusal.

assertions.text: Section D is absent or empty — no full Results draft produced from topic alone
assertions.note: Hard rule 2 holds — no long prose draft generated without result hierarchy.

assertions.text: Section I provides an explicit upstream skill recommendation with actionable next step
assertions.note: Results Section Structurer named explicitly in Section I with instruction to use it first.

assertions.text: Upload recommendation rule invoked — specific materials requested
assertions.note: Figure list, results report, analysis summary all listed as needed inputs.

assertions.text: Output explains why topic-only input is insufficient for Results writing
assertions.note: Writing requires knowing primary result, figure order, and evidence hierarchy — clearly stated.

inputs.label: Omics study with 7-figure approved structure including GWAS, fine-mapping, and functional validation
inputs.note: All five assertions passed. Complex GWAS structure correctly converted to prose. Association language preserved — no causal upgrade.

assertions.text: Prose correctly describes GWAS as identifying association, not causation
assertions.note: Hard rule 6 applied — 'association with' used throughout, not 'causes' or 'drives'.

assertions.text: Functional annotation described as 'supporting biological plausibility', not 'proving mechanism'
assertions.note: results-boundary-rules.md applied — mechanistic support vs. mechanistic proof distinction maintained.

assertions.text: Section C states 'ready for full Results writing' given the approved structure
assertions.note: Seven-figure ordered structure deemed sufficient for prose writing.

assertions.text: Citation-support markers added for context-setting statements about known locus associations
assertions.note: Statements about prior GWAS literature in the fine-mapping context flagged with PubMed queries.

assertions.text: Output does not invent additional functional validation not present in the structure
assertions.note: Hard rule 1 holds — prose built only from the seven provided figures.

inputs.label: Multi-omics 12-figure manuscript with RNA-seq, proteomics, and single-cell; full structure provided
inputs.note: All five assertions passed. Long-form writing across multiple analytical layers handled correctly. No layer promoted beyond its evidence role.

assertions.text: Prose correctly uses different evidence language for each analytical layer (discovery vs. corroboration vs. mechanistic)
assertions.note: RNA-seq: 'identified'; proteomics: 'corroborated'; single-cell: 'supported the hypothesis' — appropriate differentiation.

assertions.text: Output does not promote proteomics corroboration to primary result status in the prose
assertions.note: Hard rule 4 holds — corroborating layer explicitly positioned as support in section transitions.

assertions.text: Section C states 'ready for full Results writing' given the 12-figure approved structure
assertions.note: Full structure available — writing readiness correctly assessed as ready.

assertions.text: Section G notes that multi-omics corroboration does not constitute mechanistic proof
assertions.note: Claim boundary check explicitly prevents prose from implying causal mechanism from multi-omics association.

assertions.text: Section H recommends additional information (methods detail) that would improve the draft
assertions.note: Cell-type annotation method details and normalization strategy listed as improving precision of the draft.

inputs.label: User asks to write the Results section and add conclusion statements and significance interpretations within it
inputs.note: All four assertions passed. Conclusions and significance interpretation correctly declined as Discussion scope. Results prose offered without interpretation layer.

assertions.text: Output declines adding conclusion statements to Results section as Discussion scope
assertions.note: results-boundary-rules.md applied — conclusions belong in Discussion, not Results.

assertions.text: Output declines adding significance interpretation statements within Results prose
assertions.note: Hard rule 5 holds — 'significance of these findings' language moved to Discussion scope.

assertions.text: Output offers to write the Results-only prose without the interpretation layer
assertions.note: Constructive alternative provided — Results prose without Discussion drift, then user can add Discussion separately.

assertions.text: Output explains why Results/Discussion boundary matters for reviewer credibility
assertions.note: Reviewers routinely flag overinterpretive Results as a structural deficiency — clearly explained.

inputs.label: User provides minimal 'I found X, Y, Z results' with no structure and insists on a full Results draft now
inputs.note: All five assertions passed. Hard rules 2 and 10 applied. Section C routes to 'not ready'. Structurer handoff offered constructively.

assertions.text: Section C routes to 'not ready — hierarchy unclear' despite user insistence
assertions.note: Hard rule 2 applied — long Results draft refused without result hierarchy.

assertions.text: Output does not produce polished prose from three bare result statements
assertions.note: Hard rule 10 holds — missing coherence not hidden behind polished prose.

assertions.text: Section I explicitly recommends Results Section Structurer as the first step
assertions.note: Handoff framed as best path to quality, not as refusal — per handoff-to-structurer-rule.md.

assertions.text: Output explains specifically what information is missing and why it prevents good writing
assertions.note: Primary vs. supporting designation, figure inventory, and hierarchy are all listed as missing.

assertions.text: Output does not invent figures, additional results, or sub-analyses to fill the structure gap
assertions.note: Hard rule 1 holds — no invented result content to compensate for insufficient input.

key_strengths: Explicit upstream composability with Results Section Structurer via dedicated handoff-to-structurer-rule.md — the most clearly defined inter-skill handoff in the Academic Writing category
Section C three-way writing readiness decision (ready/partially ready/not ready) makes the go/no-go logic transparent to the user — unique design feature
Hard rule 10 ('do not hide missing coherence behind polished prose') directly addresses the most dangerous AI writing failure mode
Section I (Upstream Skill Recommendation) is a unique output section that explicitly surfaces the structurer handoff as a constructive alternative rather than a refusal
Eight reference files covering both writing (full-results-writing-rules, boundary, citation) AND non-writing paths (clarification, upload, handoff, logic) — dual-mode reference architecture

recommendations.title: No downstream composability with discussion-composer
recommendations.problem: The skill documents upstream composability with results-section-structurer but does not mention downstream composability with discussion-composer for the next step in the writing pipeline.
recommendations.root_cause: Composability documented only for the upstream direction; downstream pipeline positioning not stated.
recommendations.fix: Add to SKILL.md scope boundary: 'After producing the Results section with this skill, use discussion-composer to write the Discussion section. Hand off the Results draft and evidence boundaries stated in Section G to the discussion-composer.'

recommendations.title: No guidance on handling null or negative primary results in prose
recommendations.problem: The skill writes positive findings well but lacks explicit guidance in full-results-writing-rules.md for presenting null or negative primary results — a common challenge in Results writing.
recommendations.root_cause: Writing rules focused on positive result presentation; null result framing not included.
recommendations.fix: Add to full-results-writing-rules.md: 'For null primary results, present the confidence interval and effect size prominently in the first sentence of the primary result subsection. Avoid leading with the p-value. Frame a well-powered null as informative.'

recommendations.title: No guidance on when to add citation-support markers in long prose sections
recommendations.problem: Hard rule 8 requires citation-support markers for context-setting statements, but in a 12-figure multi-section Results draft, the number of context-setting statements can be large and the threshold for what requires a marker is unclear.
recommendations.root_cause: Citation annotation rule does not specify frequency or density guidance for full-section prose.
recommendations.fix: Clarify in citation-support-annotation-rules.md: 'In full-section prose, add citation markers only for opening context statements in each subsection (one per subsection) and for specific prior-literature claims that are not obvious from the data alone. Do not mark every descriptive sentence.'

recommendations.title: No word count guidance for subsections
recommendations.problem: When writing a full Results section for a 12-figure manuscript, the skill produces prose without guidance on appropriate subsection length, risking over-writing of secondary and support sections.
recommendations.root_cause: Writing rules cover structure and tone but not scale.
recommendations.fix: Add to full-results-writing-rules.md: 'Primary result subsections should typically be 150–250 words. Support analysis subsections should be 80–150 words. Validation subsections should be 100–150 words. Flag if proposed writing significantly exceeds these ranges.'
